entries.prompt: Think about which aspects of a current difficult situation are in your control and which are not. Use the space below to analyze a situation that's currently causing you stress and pain. How might you shift your perspective so that you can change your reaction to it?
entries.response:
Computers + procedures at work
Impatient, it is taking forever
Illogical, doesn't have to be this way
Tarnishing, my reputation is at stake
Disappointing  –  support is lacking/planning
Wasteful  –  My time is more precious
Failure  –  may cause me to fail
Scrutiny  –  places my work in question
Roadblocks  –  when I think there's progress
Out of date  –  so what else is out of date
Change Perspective
Some have answered the call and been supportive & grateful for my appreciation
Weight off shoulders
Will be most familiar in future event
Boss will appreciate effort

entries.prompt: What people think of me will have no impact on my things I value.
What behaviours have you adopted to avoid suffering or emotional pain? What do you think you have given up in order to protect yourself? What opportunities might you have missed?
entries.response:
Avoid back pain  –  no tennis, no weightlifting, no bike riding (missed competition, sociability)
Avoid calling relatives in case they are cross with me (missed connection, support)
Avoid sharing opinions (both professional and personal) in case they are ignored or rejected
Avoided inviting people over due to need to prepare house and fear of judgment from them (missed connection)
Procrastination of chores because they are built up too much

entries.prompt: If you lived more in the present moment and enjoyed yourself without projecting into the future, what might happen? How would you be a different person if you looked ahead with apatheia, or lack of fear or excessive desire?
entries.response:
I would start the first task of the chore or planning and obtaining the materials for a task. Enjoy the process of completing tasks.
Wouldn't be offended by what I felt I was entitled to (respect) or where it may lead if left unchecked.
Wouldn't feel like every moment had to be a learning experience for the future to prevent them from making mistakes.
Nothing disastrous could happen if nothing needed to be said or actioned in that moment. Time & reflection may reveal a better response or message be better received.
Demonstrated capable of changing opinion & apply critical thought.

entries.prompt: Marcus Aurelius: "The impediment to action advances action. What stands in the way becomes the way." What does that expression mean to you? Have you ever turned an obstacle into a new direction?
entries.response: Every time you have to learn something before doing a task you have a new capability and opportunities to apply elsewhere. Apply what you can do to focus on what you can't do.

entries.prompt: The learning can be the source of motivation to complete the activity.
entries.response:
To get a job I needed a particular degree which led to a qualification and career that otherwise would not have. Hernia led to obstacle that I had to lose weight for surgery.
New boss that was uncooperative, which forced a re-think to motivation and reactions to cope with then excel, discontent became content which then became satisfied.

entries.prompt: Use the space below to reframe some of the difficulties you've had as opportunities for growth, learning experiences, and positive experiences that have made you stronger.
entries.response:
Challenges - Learnings & Opportunities
New Boss - Acceptance/Appreciation
Not heard - Not rely on validation
Going overseas - Become healthy
Sell cars - Let go of past
Coeliac Disease - Watch what I eat
Lose weight - Stop drinking
Dumped by girlfriend - Become more attentive
Friend Dying - Appreciate what I have
Blood Pressure - Lessen Stress
Imposing on people - Re-connect
Father Hospitalised - Proved capable

entries.prompt: The stoics referred to a place inside ourselves called the "inner citadel." This is our retreat and the locus of our strength. It's a place where our distress and obstacles can't reach us. What can you do to build an inner citadel that you can retreat to whatever you need to tap on your own resources.
entries.response: Recognise in the moment to pause

entries.prompt: Use a breath or mantra to draw upon to trigger engaging the inner strength
entries.response:
Default to patience and curiously when faced with adversity
Fortitude in adversity will draw admiration whether you win/lose, proud/ashamed, clever/dumb

entries.prompt: Has your anticipation of the future difficulty of a situation or pursuit ever stopped you from doing something? Consider some of the ways that you can stop projecting future outcomes so that they don't prevent you from doing something in the present.
entries.response: Follow the initial impulse before there is a chance to assess the implications.

entries.prompt: When falling into daydreaming/overthinking about future, recognise and stop myself.
entries.response:
Worse case scenario is irrelevant in the moment. Only consider this when actually conducting planning.
Address immediate actions only.
Don't seek advice before starting.
Embrace imperfection.
Assume no-one will notice (good or bad)

entries.prompt: Do it for the act of it an it doesn't matter if the outcome is a waste of time.
In what ways do you think that your lack of control over your thoughts and emotions is causing difficulty in your life?
entries.response:
Daydreaming horrible events or accidents of loved ones causes stress, fear, and questioning of myself.
Cynicism about past disappointments soil potential now experiences before they have a chance to be fulfilled.
Pre-empting what someone may confront me about and arguing against it before it happens. It causes me to pre-judge people and their reactions unfairly and be unnecessarily defensive.
Thoughts of perceived disrespect make me feel worthless for several days.

entries.prompt: What are you doing on a regular basis to increase your virtue? How can you do more?
entries.response:
Acknowledge where something I did/said may have been unfair by putting myself in their shoes. Moving on.
Challenge myself to start something I have been putting off.
Look for opportunities to be in service to my team and doing tasks with pride instead of begrudgingly.
Consistently keeping with diet rules.
Everyday positivity.
Look for opportunities to practice discretion. Not say that thought.
Be resourceful by delegating where possible to team members.
